content.full_text:
April 15, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Department
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

Next month Unocal would like to launch a program whereby we invite state legislators, state administrative body officials such as members of the California Coastal Commission, and county and city officials to visit one of our offshore drilling platforms as our guests. Our intention in undertaking this program is to educate public officials and representatives about the facts about off-shore platforms and operations -- including environmental practices and safeguards, production methods and safety programs.

This would probably involve a cost to Unocal of from $100 to $300 per person (helicopters or boat trip, refreshments, etc.). We feel this would not come under the heading of a gift or honoraria as we intend it only as a tool to educate and as all state senators, assemblymen, numerous local officials, and all members of various state boards and commissions would be invited. We feel that such travel would be reasonably related to a legislative or governmental purpose or to "an issue of state, national or international public policy," and thus allowable under the Political Reform Act (Chapter 84, Stats. 1990) and other relevant laws and regulations.

We request your advice in this matter.

Very truly yours,

John L. Rafuse

JLR/dw
